mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weak supply-side capacity utilization, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in combined RSAFS/HOUST growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged decline in CPI (CPIAUCSL) below 2% indicates disinflation, prompting central bank policy accommodation and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in CPI < 2% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS/UNRATE deteriorations are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer confidence (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in RSAFS/HOUST declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.
